methods.method_name: Statistical Analysis of Bee Pollen Samples
methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the differences between pollen types and antioxidant variables in bee pollen samples, including t-tests, PCA, and cluster analysis.

steps.description: The significant differences between the pollen types identified by palynological analysis, TPC, TFC and antioxidant variables of bee pollen samples set were determined using a Student's t-test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Student's t-test to determine significant differences in pollen types and antioxidant variables

steps.description: Principal component analysis (PCA) was applied with the objective of providing a reduced interpretation of the variance of the data of the studied variables (main pollen types, TPC, TFC and antioxidant activity) in the bee pollen samples.
steps.searchable_summary: apply PCA to reduce data variance and interpret main variables
inputs: data matrix of main pollen types

steps.description: The data matrix was reduced to a small number of principal components to analyze the significant relationships between the variables.
steps.searchable_summary: reduce data matrix to principal components to analyze relationships

steps.description: Groups of pollen samples were established using multivariate cluster analysis. This statistical approach grouped samples based on a data set of variables from cases with similar characteristics.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multivariate cluster analysis to group pollen samples

steps.description: Differences between the groups were tested using the Bonferroni test through post hoc comparison (p < 0.05).
steps.searchable_summary: use Bonferroni test for post hoc comparison of group differences

steps.description: STATGRAPHICS Centurion XVI software (Statpoint Technologies, Inc., Warrenton, VA, USA) was used for treatment of data.
steps.searchable_summary: use STATGRAPHICS Centurion XVI for data processing
